raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this complaint regarding a financial loss I incurred due to a lost return shipment and the subsequent refusal of American Express to honor its purchase protection assurances. 

I purchased items using my American Express card. I returned the product because it did not match the description provided on the retailers website. The retailer issued a prepaid return label, which I used to ship the item back. The shipping method, carrier, and label were entirely provided and controlled by the retailer. 

The package, however, was lost in transit, as confirmed by the tracking information. Because the retailer never received the item, I suffered a total loss of {$630.00}. 

I contacted American Express to request assistance under their customer protection and dispute resolution policiespolicies that I relied on when choosing to use their card for the purchase. Despite this, American Express informed me that this was a third-party matter and refused to intervene. 

This characterization is incorrect. 

The retailer selected the carrier and provided the shipping label, meaning I had no control over the return method. 

The retailer did not insure the package, and I was required to use their return label. 
Because of this, the responsibility for the safe return of the package lies with the retailernot with me. 

American Expresss refusal to assist contradicts its advertised protection benefits, leaving me without the safeguards that influenced my decision to use their service.
